title: CSS Colors, Backgrounds, and Borders
description: Master CSS color systems, background styling, and border properties. Learn to create visually appealing web pages with proper color schemes, background images, and decorative borders.
learningObjectives: Understand different CSS color formats (HEX, RGB, HSL, named colors)
Apply background colors and images to elements
Control background positioning, repetition, and attachment
Style borders with various styles, widths, and colors

subtopics.title: CSS Colors
Background Colors
Background Images
Background Shorthand
subtopics.content:
The background property is a shorthand for all background properties.

**Syntax:**
```css
background: bg-color bg-image position/bg-size bg-repeat bg-origin bg-clip bg-attachment initial|inherit;
```

**Common Usage:**
```css
body {
  background: lightblue url('img_tree.png') no-repeat fixed center;
}
```

**Breakdown:**
- `lightblue` - background color
- `url('img_tree.png')` - background image
- `no-repeat` - background repeat
- `fixed` - background attachment
- `center` - background position

**Background Size:**
```css
div {
  background: url('img.jpg');
  background-size: cover; /* covers entire element */
  background-size: contain; /* fits image within element */
  background-size: 100% 100%; /* stretch to fit */
}
```

**Background Attachment:**
```css
body {
  background-attachment: fixed; /* image stays fixed when scrolling */
  background-attachment: scroll; /* image scrolls with page */
}

glossary.definition: Hexadecimal color notation using #RRGGBB format for specifying colors.
Color specification using Red, Green, and Blue values (0-255).
Color specification using Hue, Saturation, and Lightness values.
RGB color with an Alpha (transparency) channel.
An image set as the background of an HTML element.
The appearance of a border (solid, dashed, dotted, etc.).
Property that rounds the corners of an element.
Determines whether background image scrolls with the page or stays fixed.
Sets the starting position of a background image.
Controls how a background image repeats (horizontally, vertically, or not at all).
